articles.title: OpenAI claims ChatGPT’s new default model hallucinates way less
articles.summary: OpenAI's new GPT-5.5 Instant model significantly reduces hallucinations, producing 52.5% fewer false claims on high-stakes topics like medicine and law compared to previous versions.

articles.title: Meta sued by major book publishers over copyright infringement
articles.summary: Meta faces a class action lawsuit from five major book publishers and author Scott Turow for allegedly using copyrighted materials without permission to train its Llama AI models, claiming it constitutes massive copyright infringement.

articles.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
articles.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are in a high-stakes trial over OpenAI's future. Musk accuses OpenAI of abandoning its nonprofit mission to benefit humanity in favor of profit.

articles.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 Instant, a new default model for ChatGPT
articles.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 Instant as the new default model for ChatGPT, replacing the previous GPT-3.5 Instant version.

articles.title: PayPal says it’s ‘becoming a technology company again.’ That means AI.
articles.summary: PayPal is undergoing an AI-led transformation to cut $1.5B in costs through automation and job cuts while modernizing its technology infrastructure.

articles.title: Etsy launches its app within ChatGPT as it continues its AI push
articles.summary: Etsy launched a native app within ChatGPT to offer conversational shopping. The integration enables users to browse and purchase directly through ChatGPT's interface.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure competing with AWS. The funding addresses developer frustration with legacy cloud platforms' complexity and cost amid surging AI demand.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing sparks developer backlash. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers identical functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral San Francisco billboard hiring stunt featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge that attracted thousands of applicants and helped recruit engineers.

articles.title: Why Reddit blocked my daily visit to its mobile website
articles.summary: Reddit blocked mobile web access with a mandatory app overlay, forcing users to download the app without bypass options. The move restricts mobile web browsing to push app adoption.

articles.title: GameStop offers $56 billion for eBay, struggles to explain how it'll pay for it
articles.summary: GameStop made an unsolicited $55.5 billion offer to acquire eBay, proposing cost cuts and integration with its retail locations. The deal faces skepticism as eBay's market cap is four times larger, with GameStop planning debt financing and stock payment.

articles.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
articles.summary: Ubuntu and Canonical servers have been offline for over 24 hours due to a sustained cross-border cyberattack. The outage prevents OS updates and communications following a major Linux vulnerability disclosure.

articles.title: The Download: inside the Musk v. Altman trial, and AI for democracy
articles.summary: Elon Musk is suing Sam Altman, alleging he was misled about OpenAI's transition to a for-profit company. The landmark AI legal battle has begun with testimony from two of the industry's most influential figures.

articles.title: A blueprint for using AI to strengthen democracy
articles.summary: AI is becoming the primary interface for democratic participation, potentially straining institutions or addressing civic engagement and polarization depending on design choices made now.

articles.title: Week one of the Musk v. Altman trial: What it was like in the room
articles.summary: Elon Musk sued OpenAI and Sam Altman, claiming his funding was intended for a nonprofit rather than a corporation. The high-stakes trial in Oakland could impact OpenAI's planned IPO this year.

articles.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
articles.summary: AI evaluations are emerging as a major computational bottleneck in AI development, potentially limiting progress as much as compute itself has historically constrained advancement.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs are built using advanced techniques for improved performance and efficiency. The models demonstrate enhanced capabilities in reasoning and understanding complex tasks.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra is now available as an inference provider on Hugging Face, enabling users to access DeepInfra's powerful models through the Hugging Face platform for seamless AI deployment.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation. The conversation explores emerging trends and next steps for artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative content generation, streamlining workflows and enabling imaginative marketing campaigns.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available to advance farming innovation and sustainability.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners for the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition, seeking creative films exploring AI's impact on society.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in April 2026, showcasing new advancements in artificial intelligence technology and capabilities.

articles.title: Reduce friction and latency for long-running jobs with Webhooks in Gemini API
articles.summary: Google's Gemini API now supports event-driven webhooks, a push-based notification system that eliminates inefficient polling for long-running jobs, reducing friction and latency.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. The initiative explores how AI can partner with clinicians to enhance patient care outcomes.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new approach for resilient distributed AI training that improves fault tolerance and efficiency across multiple computing nodes.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, accusing them of misappropriating a charity. Evidence revealed Musk may have used OpenAI models to train xAI.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #243 discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's GPT-5.5 with coding improvements and higher pricing, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and DeepSeek V4 launch alongside AI safety concerns.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text and screenshot generation. The podcast discusses this alongside updates on Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6 models.

articles.title: Physical AI raises governance questions for autonomous systems
articles.summary: Physical AI governance becomes critical as autonomous systems integrate into robots and industrial equipment. Testing, monitoring, and controlling real-world AI actions are key challenges as robot installations double globally.

articles.title: Google made agentic AI governance a product. Enterprises still have to catch up.
articles.summary: Google launched Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform at Cloud Next '26, making agentic AI governance a native product feature. The platform includes cryptographic identity for traceability and Agent Gateway for oversight.

articles.title: SAP: How enterprise AI governance secures profit margins
articles.summary: Enterprise AI governance ensures profit margins by replacing statistical approximations with deterministic control. Organizations must prioritize precision, governance, and scalability when deploying AI in production environments.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when they occur, addressing critical challenges in collaborative AI problem-solving.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture designed to improve autonomous robot navigation in complex indoor environments by addressing localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables LLMs to handle 100 million token context windows while maintaining accuracy, significantly expanding model memory capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are inadvertently leaking sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers through a supply chain vulnerability.

articles.title: Why Gradient Descent Zigzags and How Momentum Fixes It
articles.summary: Gradient descent zigzags on uneven loss surfaces due to steep and flat regions. Momentum solves this by accumulating past gradients, accelerating progress while canceling oscillations.

articles.title: Google Adds Event-Driven Webhooks to the Gemini API, Eliminating the Need for Polling in Long-Running AI Jobs
articles.summary: Google introduced event-driven webhooks for the Gemini API, replacing inefficient polling with push-based notifications for long-running AI jobs. This eliminates wasteful requests and improves reliability for production AI pipelines.

articles.title: A Coding Guide to Survey Bias Correction Using Facebook Research Balance with IPW CBPS Ranking and Post Stratification Methods
articles.summary: Tutorial on correcting survey bias using Facebook's balance library, demonstrating IPW, CBPS, ranking, and post-stratification methods to recover unbiased population estimates through re-weighting techniques.

articles.title: 60Hz vs. 120Hz vs. 165Hz: I've tested dozens of TVs, and here's what's best for your home
articles.summary: Exploring TV refresh rates: 60Hz, 120Hz, and 165Hz options analyzed to help consumers choose the best refresh rate for their home viewing needs and preferences.

articles.title: Why this 16-inch gaming laptop is a smarter buy than a desktop in 2026
articles.summary: Dell's Alienware 16 Area-51 gaming laptop offers desktop-level performance with a mechanical keyboard and matte OLED display, making it a smarter alternative to traditional gaming desktops in 2026.

articles.title: These 5 critical Windows Defender settings are off by default - turn them on ASAP
articles.summary: Windows Defender has critical security settings disabled by default that users should enable immediately for better protection. Activating these settings is essential for maximum antivirus effectiveness.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: Non-deterministic agents produce varying outputs from identical inputs across runs. Statistical guardrails help ensure consistent and reliable behavior in these unpredictable systems.

articles.title: Agentic RAG Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Agentic RAG combines retrieval-augmented generation with autonomous agents to enable intelligent decision-making. The article explains the concept across three difficulty levels for different audience expertise.

articles.title: Effective KV Compression with TurboQuant
articles.summary: Google launched TurboQuant, a new algorithmic suite for quantizing and compressing large language models and vector search engines, essential for RAG systems.

articles.title: Discrete Time-To-Event Modeling – Predicting When Something Will Happen
articles.summary: Article introduces discrete time-to-event modeling techniques for predicting when events occur, covering fundamentals like time discretization, censoring, and life tables.

articles.title: How to Make Claude Code Validate its own Work
articles.summary: Claude Code can be improved by implementing self-validation mechanisms that allow it to verify and validate its own generated work, enhancing overall performance and reliability.

articles.title: RAG Hallucinates — I Built a Self-Healing Layer That Fixes It in Real Time
articles.summary: A developer created a lightweight self-healing layer that detects and corrects hallucinations in RAG systems in real-time, addressing reasoning failures rather than retrieval issues.

articles.title: NVIDIA and ServiceNow Partner on New Autonomous AI Agents for Enterprises
articles.summary: NVIDIA and ServiceNow partner to develop autonomous AI agents for enterprises, enabling AI systems to act safely and consistently across real workflows beyond simple generation and reasoning tasks.

articles.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
articles.summary: OpenClaw, an open-source project, gained massive popularity with over 100,000 GitHub stars by early 2026, demonstrating growing developer interest in AI agents. The Nemotron Labs series explores how open models and datasets enable organizations to build specialized AI systems on NVIDIA platforms.

articles.title: It’s Gonna Be May: 16 Games Hit the Cloud This Month, With More NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Power
articles.summary: 16 games are joining GeForce NOW this May, including AAA titles like Forza Horizon 6 and 007 First Light. The service expands RTX 5080 power to its Install-to-Play library.

articles.title: How Hapag-Lloyd uses Amazon Bedrock to transform customer feedback into actionable insights
articles.summary: Hapag-Lloyd, a major shipping company, uses Amazon Bedrock to analyze customer feedback and gain actionable insights. The company's distributed digital teams leverage AI to enhance customer experience across their global operations.

articles.title: Streamlining generative AI development with MLflow v3.10 on Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI now supports MLflow v3.10, offering enhanced capabilities for generative AI development with improved experiment tracking, observability, and evaluation features to accelerate AI workflows from experimentation to production.

articles.title: Introducing OS Level Actions in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser now supports OS-level actions, enabling AI agents to interact with system dialogs, security prompts, and native OS elements beyond the DOM layer.

articles.title: How to Build In-Vehicle AI Agents with NVIDIA: From Cloud to Car
articles.summary: NVIDIA guides developers in building AI agents for vehicles that transition from rule-based systems to intelligent, multimodal AI capable of reasoning and planning. These in-vehicle assistants move beyond simple command-response patterns to more sophisticated autonomous decision-making.

articles.title: Building for the Rising Complexity of Agentic Systems with Extreme Co-Design
articles.summary: AI is shifting from simple request-response interactions to complex agentic systems where agents autonomously call tools, spawn sub-agents, and manage memory. This requires new co-design approaches to handle increased complexity.

articles.title: Optimize Supply Chain Decision Systems Using NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills
articles.summary: NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills optimizes supply chain decisions by automating complex mathematical modeling traditionally handled by operations research teams, addressing challenges like fluctuating demand and volatile costs.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technologies and sharing them openly to reduce its environmental footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to breakthrough developments in cutting-edge tech fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel optimization, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping to improve action signals in high-dimensional visual spaces.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand LLM behavior at scale through feature attribution and interaction analysis, advancing AI interpretability and trustworthiness.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven imaging system design that uses an encoder to map objects to images, with noise corruption in measurements. An information estimator quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.
